title: Method and device to verify if a number is prime
abstract: The present disclosure proposes a method to determine if a randomly generated number n is prime. This method comprises : a. generating randomly a value s1 and a value d1, b. calculating a value dp = (n-1) * 2 â§ s1 * d1, c. calculating a value d2 and a value s2 such as dp = 2 s2 * d2, and computing a value s = s2-s1, d. initializing a first counter c1, d. generating randomly a value Î±, e. calculating a value dm=d2*Î±/d1, f. generating randomly a value A, g. initializing a second counter c2, h. calculating a value r= A dm mod n, i. verifying that the value r=1 or r=n-1, in the positive event : j. updating the first counter c1, k. if the first counter is below a first threshold T1, continuing with the step d.; I. otherwise, considering that the number n is prime; m. in the negative event : n. updating the value r = r 2 mod n; o. verifying that the value r=1, in the positive event, continuing with the step j; p. in the negative event : q. updating the second counter c2, r. if the second counter c2 is below a second threshold T2, continuing with the step n; s. otherwise, considering the number n is not prime.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. Method, executed by one or a plurality of processors, to verify and use the primality of a number n, said method comprising : a. generating randomly a value si and a value dl, b. calculating a value dp = (n-1) * 2 A sl * dl, c. calculating a value d2 and a value s2 such as dp = 2 s2 * d2, and computing a value s = s2-sl, d. initializing a first counter cl, e. generating randomly a value a, f. calculating a value dm, wherein dm=d2*a/dl, g. generating randomly a value A, h. initializing a second counter c2, i. calculating a value r= A dm mod n, j. in response to a verification that the value r=l or r=n-l, then: k. updating the first counter cl, I. if the first counter cl is below a first threshold Tl, continuing with the step e, m. otherwise, using the number n as prime number in a cryptographic operation. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein: n. in response to a verification that the value râ l and râ n-l, then: o. updating the value r = r 2 mod n, p. in response to a verification that the value r=l, then continuing with the step k. q. in response to a verification that the value râ l, then: r. updating the second counter c2, s. if the second counter c2 is below a second threshold T2, continuing with the step o. t. otherwise, discarding the number n as prime number. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the second counter c2 is defined such that c2 = s2-sl. 4. The method of any of the claims 1 to 3, further comprising: verifying that the value a is within the range of d2/4 and d2/2, in the contrary, generating randomly a new value a. 5. The method of any of the claims 1 to 4, wherein the random numbers are generated using a cryptographically secure pseudo-random generator. 6. A non-transitory machine-readable storage medium comprising instructions that, when executed by one or more processors of a computing device, cause the computing device to: a. generate randomly a value si and a value dl, b. calculate a value dp = (n-1) * 2 A sl * dl, c. calculate a value d2 and a value s2 such as dp = 2 s2 * d2, and computing a value s = s2-sl, d. initialize a first counter cl, e. generate randomly a value a, f. calculate a value dm, wherein dm=d2*a/dl, g. generate randomly a value A, h. initialize a second counter c2, i. calculate a value r= A dm mod n, j. in response to a verification that the value r=l or r=n-l, then: k. update the first counter cl, I. if the first counter is below a first threshold Tl, continue with the step e. m. otherwise, use the number n as a prime number in a cryptographic operation. 7. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 6, where the one or more processors are further to: n. in response to a verification that the value râ l and râ n-l, then: o. update the value r = r 2 mod n, 

 p. In response to a verification that the value r=l, then continue with the step k. q. in response to a verification that the value râ l, then: r. update the second counter c2, s. if the second counter c2 is below a second threshold T2, continue with the step o. t. otherwise, discard the number n is not prime. 8. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of any of the claims 6 to 7, comprising instructions that, when executed by one or more processors of a computing device, cause the processor to: define the second counter c2 such that c2 = s2-sl. 9. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of any of the claims 6, 7, or 8, comprising instructions that, when executed by one or more processors of a computing device, cause the computing device to: verify that the value a is within the range of d2/4 and d2/2, in the contrary, generating randomly a new value a. 10. Computing device, comprising one or a plurality of processors, further comprising a random number generator (226) and a cryptographic module (223), said cryptographic module being configured to, while receiving a number n: a. generate randomly a value si and a value dl, b. calculate a value dp = (n-1) * 2 A sl * dl, c. calculate a value d2 and a value s2 such as dp = 2 s2 * d2, and computing a value s = s2-sl, d. initialize a first counter cl, e. generate randomly a value a, f. calculate a value dm, g. generate randomly a value A, h. initialize a second counter c2, i. calculate a value r= A dm mod n, 

 j. verify that the value r=l or r=n-l, in the positive event: k. update the first counter cl, I. if the first counter cl is below a first threshold Tl, continue with the step e. m. otherwise, use the number n as a prime number in a cryptographic operation. 11. The computing device of claim 10, wherein the cryptographic module is further configured to: n. in the negative event: o. update the value r = r 2 mod n p. verify that the value r=l, in the positive event, continue with the step k . q. in the negative event: r. update the second counter c2, s. if the second counter c2 is below a second threshold T2, continue with the step o. t. otherwise, outputting a result representing that the number n is not prime.
